benchmark_protocol.baseline_build: A detached worktree at revision 4d170d65 built the schema-v11 benchmark executable against the same existing production vcpkg installed tree; no source files from the optimized revision were copied into that worktree.
benchmark_protocol.source_generation: Separate process per matrix. The baseline and optimized mixed-inline sources were deterministic and byte-identical: SHA-256 73ca18068426994fb595702075607d00841df2bbcd6913d0d92b8716aff534c9.
benchmark_protocol.representative_policy: Measured run nearest total_editor_ms median; earliest run breaks ties.
benchmark_protocol.verification: Every selected matrix passed its schema validation, output-plan byte accounting and planned raw compressed-payload equality checks. Representative outputs were checked with openpyxl after benchmark timing; the formula guard also verified the external hyperlink target.
benchmark_protocol.timing_policy: Only the same-machine back-to-back mixed-inline A/B is used for performance claims. Numeric and formula-plus-hyperlink elapsed values remain recorded but are excluded because unrelated local jobs caused scheduling variation.

validation.focused_tests: production 2/2 and stored 2/2 passed: worksheet event reader and public Patch cells
validation.production_ctest: 132/132 test cases passed across split sequential coverage; two monolithic attempts were invalidated by unrelated local CPU saturation at tests 96 and 98, which both passed when run directly, and tests 99-132 passed as one tail run
validation.no_images_profile: FastXLSX library plus image-disabled smoke target rebuilt and runtime smoke passed
validation.install_consumer_smoke: not rerun because this revision changes no public header, CMake, dependency, export or profile contract; the immediately preceding schema-v11 bundle records passing production, stored-only and no-images consumers

qualification: Elapsed time is system-sensitive. Claims are limited to the recorded byte-identical-source, same-machine, back-to-back mixed-inline protocol and are not generalized to arbitrary workbooks, other machines, Office compatibility, full-feature superiority or OpenXLSX superiority.
